scoring_basis: Re-score docs/experiments/02-vision-audit-pilot/runs/raw-predictions-v3.jsonl predictions against docs/experiments/05-defects-skill/labeled-defects.json refined v1 taxonomy, restricted to the 4 holdout panels.
model_prediction_source: Claude Code sub-agents loading panel + face cards + rubric_v3.md via Read tool — same as Exp 02's v3 run.
honesty_caveat: The v3 rubric was tuned by Exp 02 on the FULL 20-panel set including these 4 holdout panels. So this measurement is NOT a clean held-out test of model capability — it's a re-scoring of previously-tuned predictions against a refined taxonomy. The metric to read is: *given the model has seen these panels during tuning, does the refined taxonomy still produce the same per-category verdict?* A genuine held-out test requires a fresh labeled set from outside Exp 02's tuning loop (the chun-li-test panels would be the natural choice once their PNGs are pulled down).
rubric_used_for_scoring: detection-rubric-v1.md taxonomy (38 leaves), forward-mapped from rubric_v3.md outputs (10 flat categories).

forward_mapping_applied.lettering_error: lettering.typo_or_doubled_word OR lettering.duplicate_bubble (default chosen by note text)

outcome_per_labeled_defect.reason: v3 predicted character_count_error: false confidence: high — missed the defect
v3 flagged nothing at MED+HIGH on a GOOD panel — true negative across all categories
v3 flagged hair_discontinuity at medium; forward-mapped to character.hair_color_drift
rubric_v3 has no sequence-level category and was not given a prior_panel_image attachment
